reviews.review_msg: This class seemed easy and fun at first! but when it came to the times that we had to do the assigmnets, there was not a clear rubric in place because she says "I don't want to out you guys in the box, and you guys have to be creative", but the comment she provides is generic and not helpful and she gives the low grades back a month later
Easy course and material, but grades tough. She is not specific enough in the criteria for assignments. Avoid this prof.

Has cancelled more lectures than taught, given us ridiculous "assignments" in lieu: Watch an Amy Schumer movie. Post 250 words about Valentine's Day. Create a playlist. Hasn't marked the one assignment she has given us, and it was due a month and a half ago. Rude and demeaning. I don't know why she's teaching, she clearly has no interest in it.
Lectures were super boring - just read off a script. Tough grader and not very clear with what is required

She is a tough grader. If you speak up in class you will probably do better in the class. The assignments are manageable and she gives good feedback on how to improve. If you listen to the feedback you will be better each time. If you are not willing to work you will not be given a good grade. That only makes sense.

This class was required for my program. The class time was used to discuss our understanding of the readings and she wrote down your name when you said something even if it wasn't exactly related. I had to miss class to go to the doctor and she was not very understanding. You should definitely try to attend all classes or your grade will drop a lot

A personal issue arose for her mid-semester, so our only interactions were her pre-recorded lectures, but she seemed pretty chill. Her lectures aren't super connected to the readings, but if you have any basic knowledge of intersectional feminism, this class is a breeze. 1 midterm 1 final 3 in-class debates & tutorials account for your whole grade
She came off as kinda condescending in our email exchanges. I felt like the grading was lazy and lacked adequate feedback.
Tough grader.

Natasha is a very tough grader but I think her expectations are pretty clear in the syllabus. Her readings and lectures are interesting and discussion are very open. To everyone saying she shouldn't read lectures off paper - I have many professors that do this and I don't think it takes anything away from the lecture.

Does peer review sessions so that she can avoid giving you feedback. "Refer to your classmates' peer review of your paper." YEAH BUT YOU'RE THE PROFESSOR STUDENTS ALSO DON'T KNOW WHAT TO DO. Peer review is so that she can get away with not having to read papers early. So fricken lazy. And she's vague AF and doesn't seem confident. Doesn't give As.

Ms. Patterson ( I do not refer to her as Doctor because she doesn't deserve that credential) has changed my view of university as a whole. As a marker, she's tough, and gives awful feedback (even when requested). As a teacher, she reads off papers and discourages questions. As an academic her work is sloppy at best. Worst prof at SFU.

Dr. Patterson is BY FAR the worst professor I've ever had. She gives assignments with unclear expectations and gives terrible feedback. As this course was online, Dr. Patterson would be slow to answer emails and would direct you to "refer to the syllabus" instead of actually answering your question. Terrible TERRIBLE prof. Avoid at all costs.

She is a dictator, it's her way or the highway. She reads off papers for her lectures, doesn't allow any constructive debate, and is never clear about what she is expecting of you, in class or for the papers she assigns. She doesn't even know how to type a concrete sentence when she gives feedback.
Terrible prof. Reads lectures off a paper and is very vague! Do not recommend.
Unclear in what she expected for assignments-- and information she gave was occasionally outdated. Marking was tough and vague feedback.
Very tough marker. Extremely vague in what she expects. Does not answer questions or encourage discussion. Avoid this teacher!

Hated her. Tutorials were utterly boring and I agree with previous comments, weird personality, lack of interest in teaching, worst TA ever. Also, is she like the only TA listed here? That's further proof of how bad she is. Dear god please never let her be a professor.

Not the best TA. her marking is extremely hard, especially for a first year university course. She doesn't seem to be clear when explaining assignments/ presentations. Avoid unless you want your GPA to drop.

She is the worst TA I ever had. Doesn't help you with anything, and if she does, it's very vague. Her markings are ridiculously hard and you cannot question her about them. She makes no effort in explaining or discussing questions you may have and treats you poorly. How this woman is a PhD student, I don't know.

Horrible Horrible TA. She wants you to fail on the course and she has very weird personality. She doesn't seem she's interested in teaching and she's always complaining her low wage as a TA. All I can say to you is.. Avoid, Avoid her.
SUCH a difficult marking TA, avoid at ALL costs
